categories.name: Biological
categories.description: Food scraps, bones, and other animal- or food-derived organic waste that decomposes naturally.
categories.handling: Place in the wet/green bin for composting. Keep free of plastic wrappers and packaging.

categories.name: Cardboard
categories.description: Corrugated boxes and thick paperboard packaging that can be recycled multiple times.
categories.handling: Flatten boxes and keep them dry. Place in the dry recycling stream.

categories.name: Glass
categories.description: Bottles and jars made of glass — infinitely recyclable without loss of quality.
categories.handling: Rinse and place in the glass recycling stream. Handle broken shards with care.

categories.name: Metal
categories.description: Aluminium and steel cans, foils, and small metal objects.
categories.handling: Rinse and place in the dry recycling stream. Crush cans to save space.

categories.name: Miscellaneous
categories.description: Mixed or non-recyclable items that do not fit any other category.
categories.handling: Place in general waste. Avoid mixing with recyclables to prevent contamination.

categories.name: Paper
categories.description: Office paper, newspaper, and other thin paper products.
categories.handling: Keep dry and clean. Place in the dry recycling stream.

categories.name: Plastic
categories.description: Bottles, containers, and packaging made of plastic resins.
categories.handling: Rinse, dry, and place in the plastic recycling stream where accepted.

categories.name: Textile
categories.description: Clothing, fabric, and other woven materials.
categories.handling: Donate if usable, otherwise take to a textile collection point.

categories.name: Vegetation
categories.handling: Compost or place in the green/garden waste stream.

quiz.question: Which category does a banana peel belong to?
quiz.explanation: Food-derived waste like fruit peels is Biological and belongs in the wet/compost bin.

quiz.question: A flattened Amazon delivery box should be sorted as?
quiz.explanation: Corrugated delivery boxes are Cardboard. Flatten them and keep them dry for recycling.

quiz.question: An empty aluminium soda can goes in which stream?
quiz.explanation: Cans are Metal. Rinse and place them in the metal/dry recycling stream.

quiz.question: What is the best handling for a worn-out but clean T-shirt?
quiz.explanation: Textiles should be donated or reused. Take unusable fabric to a textile collection point.

quiz.question: A rinsed glass jam jar belongs to?
quiz.explanation: Glass jars are infinitely recyclable. Rinse and place them in the glass recycling stream.

quiz.question: Garden leaves and grass clippings are categorised as?
quiz.explanation: Plant and garden waste is Vegetation and can be composted or placed in garden waste.

quiz.question: An empty shampoo bottle should be sorted as?
quiz.explanation: Shampoo bottles are Plastic. Rinse and place them in the plastic recycling stream.

quiz.question: Yesterday's newspaper goes in which category?
quiz.explanation: Newspaper is Paper. Keep it dry and clean for the dry recycling stream.

quiz.question: A greasy pizza box should be handled as?
quiz.explanation: Food-soiled cardboard is contaminated and usually cannot be recycled — it goes to general waste.
